Funnel Algebra. So let's get jiggy with it. So we just covered the sequence strategy, making sure that we're matching the objective to the type of funnel. And now we're going over number two, which is funnel math for buying traffic. I say EPCs here, but that's just shorthand for me to remember what we're gonna go over. So there's some definitions that we're gonna need to go over and things that you're going to need to understand. The first is EPCs, which is earning per click, which is how much you actually making per click that you get. This is a really big affiliate marketer stat that they track because they're huge traffic guys. And so the reason earning per click is so important is because it will allow you to, independent of platforms, figure out how much you can spend per click to drive traffic to a page, right? Obviously the type of traffic where that audience is is gonna matter a lot in terms of what your earnings per click are. In the colder the audience, the lower the earning per click tends to be, but that's where having a really strong funnel process and follow-up is so important. All right, so earnings per click is EPCs. CPM is cost per thousand. M is like the Roman, you know, which is basically the gross revenue that's generated off the ad spend and then ROI, which is mistakenly many times said as, many people say their ROI when it really means the RO has the ROA. And the ROI is the actual return on investment when you're taking into account your profit, okay, which is gonna be the real driver of how you can market. All right, so here's a visual example of how this works. So you've got your dollars here, you buy CPMs, you buy impressions, okay, and then those impressions that you're buying are displaying an ad like this one. All right, your CTR is the amount of people who click, the percentage of people who click that ad once they see it. All right, and then those people go to the page and then you have your opt-in percentage, all right. This is how you reverse engineer what your actual cost per litre is. So a lot of people don't understand where their actual cost per litre is, right. This is actually how it works. All right, after that you'll have your percentage of opt-ins that you converted to sales and then you'll have your average ticket that you'll, you should already know and then you'll be able to calculate your return on ad spend, then you'll be able to get your EPCs and then you get your ROI, which relates back to the dollar, right, that you're actually spending and then you get your real EPCs, which is what I actually track, what actually should matter to you and then your real max cost per litre. Okay, so the numbers before this tend to be more vanity and the last three tend to be reality. Those are the real ones that drive how much you can spend, okay. So let's walk through an example together. So let's say you spend $100 on ads, okay, and it's $25 per CPM, all right, it says $25 per thousand eyeballs. That means that if you spend $100 you get four times the thousand, right, which is 4,000 impressions, okay, so hopefully you're tracking with me because we had $100, it's $25 per thousand, which means we get 25 times 4 to get that $100, which gets us four times a thousand impressions, which is 4,000, okay. Now, if we know that we have a 3% click-through rate, that means 3% of that 4,000 are actually clicking through on our ad, which means we would get 120 clicks, that's the second number there, all right, so 120 clicks actually go to this page, which is our opt-in page, and let's say we're converting 20% of our opt-ins, of traffic hits the page, which means we have 24 opt-ins, and that would give us $4 per lead, all right. So does everyone understand how you get from $100 to $4 per lead, all right. It's not perfectly $4 because it's $24, but it's almost $4 per lead here, okay. Hopefully this may, it's like $4.05. Hopefully this makes sense to everyone. You get $100, your CPM, you get 4 of your thousand to get 4,000, 3% of those 4,000 eyeballs click through, which is 120 clicks, of the 120 clicks, 20% of those convert into opt-ins, right. Of those opt-ins, you convert 20% as sales, which means you have five sales from your $100, right, and if your average ticket is $600, then it means you made $3,000, which would give you a 30 to 1 row as, which is phenomenal, right. And this is where everyone gets messed up, okay. So then they're like, oh, I have $25 EPCs. That's crazy. The reality is most affiliate marketers and things like that, they track and they only sell information products, which basically have almost zero cost to them. But we, you and I, both are probably in the service business. And so service does have costs, lots of them, right. And so if you have a 20% margin that you're running in your business, your actual ROI was six to one, not 30 to one, right. And so this is where people get trippy. This is where things get messed up where they're like, I don't understand why my campaigns aren't working. I should be making X and I'm not, right. And so your real EPCs are $5 a click, meaning that is as high as you can go before you start losing money, right. And then for you, if you're tracking your cost per lead, then it means your real max cost per lead would be $24. Okay. Now for most of you, that makes a lot of sense because that's truly where the max is where you wouldn't really be making any more money, which is why a lot of people when they have, you know, this type of process can't get past that threshold. Obviously there's lots of things you can do to increase the average ticket, adding supplements, adding food, adding paid and fools at the end, adding high ticket upsells, similar privates, things like that, right, to increase your average ticket from 600 beyond. But those are the things in the sales process that can affect how you buy traffic. But these are the things that lead up to this point. So inform your decisions in terms of how you buy media. Okay. So the last three here are the real power players. Okay. Those are the real numbers that are driving your decisions for this whole game, right. And so now that we've established what these numbers are, you now can buy traffic on any platform knowing this. Okay. And so now that we know that they are, here's how we can improve them.
